initial_program:
% Choose the most frequent nonzero colour (the mode)
1 { mode(M) : color(M), M != 0 } 1 :- true.

% The chosen colour must appear at least once
dummyMode(M) :- mode(M).
:- dummyMode(M), count(M), count(M) > 0.

% Helper predicates for nonzero rows / columns
rowHasNonZero(R) :- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0.
colHasNonZero(C) :- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Dummy predicates to make the aggregate variables safe
dummyRowR(R)   :- rowHasNonZero(R).
dummyRowMax(R) :- rowHasNonZero(R).
dummyColC(C)   :- colHasNonZero(C).
dummyColMax(C) :- colHasNonZero(C).

% Bounding box of nonzero cells
minRow = #min{R : rowHasNonZero(R)} :- dummyRowR(R).
maxRow = #max{R : rowHasNonZero(R)} :- dummyRowMax(R).
minCol = #min{C : colHasNonZero(C)} :- dummyColC(C).
maxCol = #max{C : colHasNonZero(C)} :- dummyColMax(C).

% Count occurrences of each colour 1..9
count(1) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,1)}.
count(2) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,2)}.
count(3) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,3)}.
count(4) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,4)}.
count(5) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,5)}.
count(6) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,6)}.
count(7) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,7)}.
count(8) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,8)}.
count(9) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,9)}.

% No colour has a strictly larger count than the mode
higherExists = #count{C : color(C), C != 0, C != M, count(C) > count(M)} :- dummyMode(M), color(C).
hasHigher    :- higherExists, higherExists > 0.
:- not hasHigher.

% Two 44 pattern blocks
block1(R,C) :- row(R), col(C),
                R >= minRow, R <= minRow + 3,
                C >= minCol, C <= minCol + 3.
block2(R,C) :- row(R), col(C),
                R >= maxRow + 3, R <= maxRow + 6,
                C >= maxCol + 3, C <= maxCol + 6.

% Output the mode colour in the two pattern blocks
output(R,C,M) :- block1(R,C), mode(M).
output(R,C,M) :- block2(R,C), mode(M).

% All other cells are 0
output(R,C,0) :- row(R), col(C), not block1(R,C), not block2(R,C).

% Define the output grid size (99)
row(R) :- R = 0..8.
col(C) :- C = 0..8.

#show output/3.
initial_errors: <block>:36:35-36: error: syntax error, unexpected ",", expecting } or ;

1 { mode(M) : color(M), M != 0 } 1 :- true.

% The chosen colour must appear at least once
dummyMode(M) :- mode(M).
:- dummyMode(M), count(M), count(M) > 0.

% Helper predicates for nonzero rows / columns
rowHasNonZero(R) :- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0.
colHasNonZero(C) :- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Dummy predicates to make the aggregate variables safe

dummyRowMax(R) :- rowHasNonZero(R).
dummyColC(C)   :- colHasNonZero(C).
dummyColMax(C) :- colHasNonZero(C).

% Bounding box of nonzero cells
minRow = #min{R : rowHasNonZero(R)} :- dummyRowR(R).
maxRow = #max{R : rowHasNonZero(R)} :- dummyRowMax(R).
minCol = #min{C : colHasNonZero(C)} :- dummyColC(C).
maxCol = #max{C : colHasNonZero(C)} :- dummyColMax(C).

% Count occurrences of each colour 1..9
count(1) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,1)}.
count(2) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,2)}.
count(3) = #count{R,C : input(R,C,3)}.

% No colour has a strictly larger count than the mode

hasHigher    :- higherExists, higherExists > 0.
:- not hasHigher.

% Two 44 pattern blocks
block1(R,C) :- row(R), col(C),
                R >= minRow, R <= minRow + 3,
                C >= minCol, C <= minCol + 3.
block2(R,C) :- row(R), col(C),

% Output the mode colour in the two pattern blocks
output(R,C,M) :- block1(R,C), mode(M).
output(R,C,M) :- block2(R,C), mode(M).

% All other cells are 0
output(R,C,0) :- row(R), col(C), not block1(R,C), not block2(R,C).
